revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Iwafune-jinja (Konan)]][[ja:天忍穂別神社]] {{Expand Japanese|天忍穂別神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Iwafune Shrine, Konan | native_name = 天忍穂別神社 | image = Iwafune-jinja (Konan), haiden-1.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Iwafune Shrine, Konan | coordinates = {{coord|33.586771|133.781211}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Iwafune Shrine, Konan'''|天忍穂別神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tosa Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11423010|ltq=Q11423010|qid=Q11423010|1=List of Shikinaisha in Tosa Province}} ** Series ordinal: 4 ** P155: {{ill|Sakamotono Shrine|lt=Sakamotono Shrine|qid=Q135041449|1=Sakamotono Shrine (Q135041449)}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135041450|ltq=Q135041450|qid=Q135041450|1=Komatsuno Shrine (Q135041450)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q877209|ltq=Q877209|qid=Q877209|1=Kōnan, Kōchi}} * {{ill|Tosa Province|lt=Tosa Province|ar|دوشو|cs|Tosa (provincie)|de|Provinz Tosa|en|Tosa Province|es|Provincia de Tosa|fa|ولایت توسا|fi|Tosan provinssi|fr|Province de Tosa|gl|Provincia de Tosa|id|Provinsi Tosa|it|Tosa (provincia)|ja|土佐国|ko|도사국|lt|Tosos žemė|nl|Tosa (provincie)|pt|Província de Tosa|ru|Тоса (провинция)|sh|Tosa (provincija)|simple|Tosa Province|sk|Tosa (provincia)|sr|Провинција Тоса|th|แคว้นโทซะ|tr|Tosa (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Тоса|zh_min_nan|Tosa-kok|zh|土佐國|qid=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}} * {{ill|Q11667715|ltq=Q11667715|qid=Q11667715|1=Kami District, Kōchi}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|33.586771|133.781211}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Iwafune-jinja (Konan), haiden-1.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183272 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 6490005005007 == Commons category (P373) == * Iwafune-jinja (Konan) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県香南市香我美町山川字隅ヶ迫', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Iwafune-jinja (Konan)}} [[JA:天忍穂別神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65259139}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Amaneoshihowake Shrine |Image=[[File:Iwafune-jinja (Konan), haiden-1.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location={{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|qid=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kōnan City|Q877209|qid=Q877209|1=Kōnan, Kōchi}} Kagami-chō Yamakawa, Aza Sumigasako |Coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|13.1|N|133|46|52.4|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Amaneoshihowake Shrine}} |Enshrined deity={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object= |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded= |Chief priest= |Main hall= |Alternative name=Ishifune Shrine |Pilgrimage affiliation= |Annual festival= |Rituals= |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Amaneoshihowake
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Iwafune-jinja (Konan), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Amaneoshihowake Shrine''' (あめのおしほわけじんじゃ / あまのおしほわけじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Yamakawa, {{ill|lt=Kagami-chō|Q6346338|qid=Q6346338|1=Kagami, Kōchi (Kami)}}, {{ill|lt=Kōnan City|Q877209|qid=Q877209|1=Kōnan, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|qid=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (あめのおしほみみのみこと) – principal deity {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (にぎはやひのみこと) – enshrined subsidiary deity; son of Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto. * Sub-shrine within the precincts: Tanabata Shrine. Tanabatatsume (たなばたつめ) = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Yorozu-Takuhata-Chijihime|1=UNKNOWN}} (あめのよろずたくはたちぢひめ) = {{ill|lt=Takuhatachijihime-no-Mikoto|Q11110848|qid=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}} (たくはたちぢひめ), the wife of Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto. === History === * The date of establishment is unknown. It predates the completion of the Engishiki in 927 (Engen 5). * During the Edo period it was called “Oishifune-no-miya” and “Ishifune Daimyōjin.” A record dated April 15, Genkō 4 (1324) refers to Ishifune Daimyōjin Shrine. In Hōei 2 (1705) it was renamed Amaneoshihowake Shrine. In Meiji 5 it was designated a prefectural shrine. According to legend, Ame-no-Oshihowake-no-Mikoto, father of Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, descended to this land of Yamakawa in Higashikawa Village and enshrined himself there. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, longing for his father deity, flew down riding a natural stone boat measuring eight shaku by three shaku and was enshrined together with him. The legendary stone boat still exists today. * According to the {{ill|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|Q2702542|qid=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}, compiled during the Shōhei era (931–938) under the ritsuryō system, Kagami-chō Yamakawa was recorded as part of Ōshi-go in Kami District, suggesting that the entirety of present-day Kagami-chō was included in Ōshi-go. (Kōchi Prefecture Kami District Jūman Site Excavation Report){{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Detailed Search - National Archaeological Site Report Database|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/search?all=%E5%8D%81%E4%B8%87%E9%81%BA%E8%B7%A1%E7%99%BA%E6%8E%98%E8%AA%BF%E6%9F%BB%E5%A0%B1%E5%91%8A%E6%9B%B8|website=sitereports.nabunken.go.jp|accessdate=2019-05-10}} * In the Qinzan Collection written by {{ill|lt=Tani Jinzan|Q63566583|qid=Q63566583|1=Tani Jinzan}} (personal name Shigetō) of Tosa Nankaku scholarship, dated Hōei 2 (1705), it states that the shrine lay about two ri north of Yasu Town, in Yamakawa of Higashikawa Village, and Volume 14, “Examination of Shikinaisha in Tosa Province,” records the investigation results when it was a prefectural shrine. * Two ridge tags remain extant: one inscribed “Oishifune Shrine, 4th year of Shōwa, second month, day unknown (1315), Mononobe no Suechika,” and another inscribed “Oishifune Shrine, second day of the second month, Tenshō 6 (1578), Patron Mononobe no Suenobu Mansuke-jō, Jin’emon-jō.” * Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was registered as a prefectural shrine in Meiji 5, but was later reclassified as a gōsha.{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Kōchi Prefecture Shrine Register|url=http://www.shimanto-chimei.com/地名データブック/高知県神社明細帳/|website=Shimanto Town Place Name Dictionary|accessdate=2019-05-10|language=ja-JP}} === Legends === * Explanatory sign by the Kagami Town Board of Education: Long ago, a deity named Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, said to be a grandchild of Amaterasu Ōmikami, rode upon a stone boat and soared through the sky, arriving at the mountain peak of Sumigasako in Yamakawa. Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto first descended upon a mountain in Kawachi Province, then stayed at the foot of Momoo-yama in Yamato Province, and eventually, longing for his father deity Ame-no-Oshihomimi-no-Mikoto, arrived in Tosa Province. His first descent in Tosa was at Kamiokayama (Noichi) near the lower reaches of the Monobe River. From there he passed through Tomike Village, Nishikawa Village, the hamlet of Nagatani, Funato Pass, Suenobu’s Mizufune, and Funatani in Yamakawa, finally arriving at the present shrine in Sumigasako. The stone at Maikawa is said to bear traces of bugaku performed when the deity rested, and in the hamlet of Nagatani there is an Eboshi Rock where he is said to have hung his eboshi cap. The southern valley of the current shrine is called Kattori, where he removed his crown, while the northern valley is called Tsue-dani, where he placed his spear. Nearby Kubisago is said to be where he placed his necklace. The stone boat on which Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto rode lies behind the shrine precincts and takes the form of a gigantic natural stone shaped like a boat. In addition, many small stone boats donated by sailors are piled nearby. As these numerous small stone boats attest, the deity is known both as a guardian of maritime safety and as a deity protecting against smallpox. From Kazuo Katsurai, “Tosa Legends.” === Shrine precincts === File:Iwafune-jinja (Konan), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Iwafune-jinja (Konan), haiden-3.jpg|Haiden File:Iwafune-jinja (Konan), Tanabata-jinja.jpg|Tanabata Shrine within the precincts File:Iwafune-jinja (Konan), hinoki.jpg|Japanese cypress (designated natural monument of Kōnan City) File:Iwafune-jinja (Konan), sandou.jpg|Stone steps of the approach === Rituals === * Autumn festival on November 15 === Cultural properties === * Historic site ** Amaneoshihowake Shrine (Ishifune Shrine) – designated April 1, 1978 (Shōwa 53). * Natural monument ** Japanese cypress of Ishifune Shrine – designated July 3, 2019 (Reiwa 1). === Other === * Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto took as his wife {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tomiyasubime|1=UNKNOWN}} (called Mimakiyahime in the Nihon Shoki), the younger sister of Nagasunehiko, and they had {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Umashimaji-no-Mikoto is regarded as the ancestor of the Mononobe no Muraji, Hozumi no Omi, and Uneme no Omi clans. In the area around this shrine are the {{ill|lt=Monobe River|Q11570692|qid=Q11570692|1=Monobe River}} and the places {{ill|lt=Yasu Town (Kōchi Prefecture)|Q8049931|qid=Q8049931|lt=Yasu Town|1=Yasu, Kōchi}} and {{ill|lt=Umaji Village|Q1206380|qid=Q1206380|1=Umaji, Kōchi}}, whose pronunciations resemble “Yasu” and “Umashi.” * The stone boat said to have been ridden by Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto lies behind the shrine precincts and is a gigantic natural stone shaped like a boat. Nearby, numerous small stone boats donated by sailors are scattered about. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat-inline|Iwafune-jinja (Konan)}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Iwafune Shrine, Konan]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iwafune Shrine, Konan]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kōnan, Kōchi]] [[Category:香南市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:高知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kōnan, Kōchi]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
